Class ShipBoard

java.lang.Object
Model.Ship.ShipBoard
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable
public class ShipBoard extends Object implements Serializable, Cloneable
Full-featured ShipBoard for Galaxy Trucker. Manages placement, connections, rotations, integrity, firepower, thrust, shields, damage, and exposed connectors.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Details

    • ShipBoard

      public ShipBoard()

  • Method Details

    • getCondensedShip

      public CondensedShip getCondensedShip()

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid()

    • setValid

      public void setValid(boolean valid)

    • addComponent

      public void addComponent(SpaceshipComponent component, Coordinates coordinates) throws InvalidMethodParameters
      Places a spaceship component on the board at the specified coordinates. Ensures the placement is within board bounds, the position is unoccupied, and the component is properly connected to existing components via compatible connectors.
      Parameters:
      component - The spaceship component to add.
      coordinates - The position on the board where the component should be placed.
      Throws:
      InvalidMethodParameters - if the position is invalid, already occupied, or not connected properly.

    • isAdjacentToExistingComponent

      public boolean isAdjacentToExistingComponent(Coordinates coordinates)
      Checks if a position is adjacent to an existing component on the board.
      Parameters:
      coordinates - The position on the board to check.
      Returns:
      true if the component can be placed, false otherwise.

    • removeComponent

      public void removeComponent(Coordinates coordinates) throws InvalidMethodParameters
      Removes a spaceship component from the board at the given coordinates. Translates logical coordinates into grid indices and clears the position if it contains a component. Throws an exception if coordinates are out of bounds.
      Parameters:
      coordinates - the location from which to remove the component
      Throws:
      InvalidMethodParameters - if the coordinates are invalid

    • isValidPosition

      private boolean isValidPosition(int row, int col)
      Checks whether the given row and column indices are within the bounds of the ship grid.
      Parameters:
      row - the row index to check
      col - the column index to check
      Returns:
      true if the indices are within bounds, false otherwise

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      DOES NOT Checks whether the ship board is entirely empty. Does check if there are no components but in central cabin position
      Returns:
      true if all cells in the board are null, false otherwise

    • isConnectedToExistingComponents

      public boolean isConnectedToExistingComponents(SpaceshipComponent component, int row, int col)
      Checks if the given component can be connected to any existing component on the board by verifying adjacent connectors for compatibility.
      Parameters:
      component - the component to place
      row - the target row in the board grid
      col - the target column in the board grid
      Returns:
      true if there is at least one compatible adjacent connector, false otherwise

    • getOffset

      private int[] getOffset(Side side)
      Returns the relative coordinate offset for a given side of a component. Used to find neighboring positions in the grid.
      Parameters:
      side - the side of the component (FRONT, REAR, LEFT, RIGHT)
      Returns:
      an array containing the offset as [rowDelta, colDelta]

    • getOppositeSide

      private Side getOppositeSide(Side side)
      Returns the opposite side of the given side. Used to determine the side of a neighboring component for connection checks.
      Parameters:
      side - the original side
      Returns:
      the side opposite to the input

    • connectosAreCompatible

      private boolean connectosAreCompatible(ConnectorType a, ConnectorType b)
      Checks if two connector types are compatible. NONE is not compatible with anything, UNIVERSAL is compatible with everything except NONE, SINGLE is compatible with itself and UNIVERSAL, DOUBLE is compatible with itself and UNIVERSAL.
      Parameters:
      a - the first connector type
      b - the second connector type
      Returns:
      true if the connectors are compatible, false otherwise

    • connectorsAreConnected

      private boolean connectorsAreConnected(ConnectorType a, ConnectorType b)
      Determines if two connectors are connected with each other. (this means that they are compatible) NONE is not connected with anything Universal connectors are compatible with any connector,
      Parameters:
      a - the first connector
      b - the second connector
      Returns:
      true if connectors are compatible, false otherwise

    • setActiveComponent

      public void setActiveComponent(SpaceshipComponent component)
      Sets the active component currently being manipulated or selected.
      Parameters:
      component - the component to mark as active

    • getActiveComponent

      public SpaceshipComponent getActiveComponent()
      Retrieves the currently active component.
      Returns:
      the active component instance, or null if none is set

    • checkIntegrity

      public boolean checkIntegrity()
      Checks whether the ship is still structurally connected after potential damage. Uses depth-first search to ensure all components are connected starting from the first one found.
      Returns:
      true if all components are reachable and the ship is whole, false otherwise

    • areComponentsConnected

      public boolean areComponentsConnected(SpaceshipComponent a, SpaceshipComponent b)

    • dfs

      private int dfs(int row, int col, boolean[][] visited)
      Performs a depth-first search to count connected components starting from a given cell.
      Parameters:
      row - the row to start from
      col - the column to start from
      visited - a matrix to mark visited cells
      Returns:
      the total number of connected components found

    • getShipBoundaries

      public int[] getShipBoundaries()
      Calculates the rectangular boundary of the ship on the grid. Iterates through all cells to find the minimum and maximum rows and columns where components are placed. Returns null if the ship is empty.
      Returns:
      an array of four integers: [minRow, maxRow, minCol, maxCol], or null if empty

    • getAllComponents

      public List<SpaceshipComponent> getAllComponents()
      Returns a list of all components currently placed on the ship board. Iterates through the grid and collects all non-null components.
      Returns:
      a list of all spaceship components on the board

    • getComponent

      public SpaceshipComponent getComponent(Coordinates coordinates)
      Retrieves the component located at the given coordinates. Converts logical coordinates to grid indices and returns the component if within bounds.
      Parameters:
      coordinates - the logical board coordinates
      Returns:
      the component at the specified location, or null if out of bounds or empty

    • getIndex

      public Coordinates getIndex(SpaceshipComponent goalComponent)
      Searches for the specified component in the grid and returns its coordinates. If not found, returns (-1, -1).
      Parameters:
      goalComponent - the component to locate on the board
      Returns:
      the coordinates of the component or (-1, -1) if not found

    • printOccupiedMatrix

      public void printOccupiedMatrix()
      Prints a visual matrix of the ship grid to the console. Displays '[X]' for occupied positions and '[ ]' for empty ones within the active ship boundaries.

    • validateShip

      public boolean validateShip()
      Validates the ship by checking: - Connector compatibility - Blocking of engines and cannons - Proper orientation of engines and cannons - Special rules for cabins, alien support, shields, and special cargo
      Returns:
      true if the ship is valid, false otherwise

    • getExposedConnectors

      public Map<Coordinates,List<Side>> getExposedConnectors()
      Identifies all spaceship components on the board with exposed connectors. A connector is considered exposed if it is adjacent to an empty cell, out-of-bounds space, or incompatible connector. The method uses flood-fill to distinguish interior voids from actual exposure to external space.
      Returns:
      a map linking each exposed component position to a list of its exposed sides

    • getTotalExposedConnectorCount

      public int getTotalExposedConnectorCount()
      Utility method to count the total number of exposed connectors across all components. This method should be used when counting individual exposed connectors rather than just the number of components with exposed connectors.
      Returns:
      the total count of individual exposed connectors

    • reserveComponent

      public void reserveComponent(SpaceshipComponent component)
      Adds a component to the reserved components list. Throws an exception if more than 2 are reserved.
      Parameters:
      component - the component to reserve

    • getReservedComponents

      public List<SpaceshipComponent> getReservedComponents()
      Returns a copy of the reserved components list.
      Returns:
      list of reserved components

    • removeReservedComponent

      public void removeReservedComponent(int index)
      Removes a reserved component by index.
      Parameters:
      index - the index to remove

    • purgeDisconnectedComponents

      public void purgeDisconnectedComponents()
      Removes all components that are no longer connected to the central part of the ship. Performs a DFS from the center and removes anything not visited.

    • render

      public void render(MatchLevel level)

    • renderEmpty

      public String[] renderEmpty()

    • renderNull

      public String[] renderNull()

    • clone

      public ShipBoard clone()
      Overrides:
      clone in class Object

    • renderEmptyBig

      public String[] renderEmptyBig()
      Renders an empty slot with the same dimensions as renderBig().
      Returns:
      array of 5 strings representing an empty big slot
